data.title: CTS Corporation beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• CTS Corporation press release (NYSE:CTS): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.57 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $135.31M (+4.0% Y/Y) beats by $2.66M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin was 23.0%, up from 21.7% in the second quarter of 2024.
  • Operating cash flow was $28 million, compared to $20 million in the second quarter of 2024.
  • 2025 Guidance

    • Assuming the continuation of current market conditions, CTS is maintaining its guidance of sales in the range of $520-$550 million vs. $533.19M consensus and adjusted diluted EPS to be in the range of $2.20-$2.35 vs. $2.20 consensus.

data.title: JPMorgan, Bank of America subpeonaed by House over Chinese IPO - report
data.description:
A House committee focusing on the national security threat posed by China demanded that JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) and Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) produce documents related to their roles in a huge Chinese battery company's initial public offering, according to a media report.

The House of Representatives Select Committee on the Chinese Communist Party subpoenaed the companies' chief executives after the banks helped Contemporary Amperex Technology (OTC:CYATY) (OTC:CTATF), or CATL, list in Hong Kong in May, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing letters it had reviewed.

The committee, which had urged JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM) and BofA  (NYSE:BAC) in April to pull out of the deal, said the banks had failed to produce information related to the IPO that it had previously requested.

CATL, which supplies batteries to electric vehicle makers like Tesla (TSLA), had been added to Department of Defense list that identifies companies as military in nature. The committee had said that participating in the IPO presented "serious regulatory, financial, and reputation risks" for the banks.

Bank of America (BAC) and JPMorgan (JPM) continued with the IPO, which raised $5.2B, the largest IPO this year, the report said.

In an interview on Bloomberg Television in May, JPMorgan (JPM) CEO and Chair Jamie Dimon said the bank "did a lot of due diligence around the issues that people raised. If we thought it was wrong, we wouldn't do it."

A Bank of America (BAC) spokesperson said the company would continue to engage with the committee.

CATL said in a prospectus that it believes its inclusion in the Department of Defense list is a mistake, and that it's talking with the DoD to address the issue.

"It does not restrict us from conducting business with entities other than a small number of U.S. governmental authorities, thus is expected to have no substantial adverse impact on our business," CATL said in the prospectus.

data.title: Everything Blockchain moves forward with MemeStrategy spin-off
data.description:
Everything Blockchain (OTCPK:EBZT) Thursday announced the next stage in its MemeStrategy spin-off, formally initiating the S-1 registration process and preparing for a potential NASDAQ uplisting through a capital raise and strategic acquisition.

Under the current plan, EBZT will distribute 1 share of MemeStrategy for every 6 shares of EBZT held, contingent on SEC registration.

The company previously announced its intent to create MemeStrategy, and is now taking concrete steps to structure it as the first public meme asset treasury vehicle, modeled after MicroStrategy but tailored to the crypto memecoin economy.

data.title: OSR Holdings enters term sheet to acquire Woori IO
data.description:
OSR Holdings (NASDAQ:OSRH) has signed a term sheet with Woori IO Co, a South Korean medical device company developing next-generation noninvasive glucose monitoring technology.

The agreement sets forth certain key terms for a strategic acquisition of WORIO by OSR Holdings' (NASDAQ:OSRH) Korean affiliate, OSR Holdings Co.

Under the proposed structure, WORIO will become a wholly owned subsidiary of OSRK.

Shareholders of WORIO will receive newly issued shares of OSRK through the share exchange and may be eligible to convert them into OSRH common stock traded on NASDAQ, subject to certain conditions, including the "$10 per OSRH share" condition.

data.title: Union Pacific Non-GAAP EPS of $3.03 beats by $0.13, revenue of $6.15B in-line
data.description:
• Union Pacific press release (NYSE:UNP): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.03 beats by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $6.15B (+2.5% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Operating ratio of 59.0% and adjusted OR* of 58.1%
  • Revenue carloads up 4%
  • Affirming 2025 Outlook:
  • No change to long-term capital allocation strategy- Capital plan of $3.4 billion- Share repurchases of $4.0 to $4.5 billion- Third quarter 2025 dividend increase of 3%

data.title: Digital Realty partners with Oracle to help companies accelerate hybrid IT, AI adoption
data.description:
Data center REIT Digital Realty (NYSE:DLR) announced on Thursday a partnership with Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) to help companies accelerate hybrid information technology and artificial intelligence adoption.

DLR shares were +0.66% pre-market to $180.42, while ORCL was 0.48% higher to $243.05.

data.title: Cenovus Energy preparing competing bid for MEG Energy - report
data.description:
Cenovus Energy (NYSE:CVE) is preparing a bid for MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF), setting up a potential challenge to Strathcona Resources' (OTCPK:STHRF) hostile takeover offer, the Financial Post reported late Wednesday.

MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF) has set a Monday deadline for companies to submit bids to buy the oil sands producer, according to the report, which also said Cenovus (NYSE:CVE) is believed to be seeking financing to support the bid, although there is no guarantee it will proceed.

If Cenovus (CVE) proceeds with a bid, it would represent a significant escalation in the battle for control of MEG (OTCPK:MEGEF), which began in May with Strathcona's (OTCPK:STHRF) announcement of a $5.9B cash and stock takeover offer.

MEG (OTCPK:MEGEF) urged shareholders to reject Strathcona's (OTCPK:STHRF) proposal and launched a strategic review of alternatives, including an invitation of potential rival bids for the company.

Analysts at RBC Capital have said Cenovus (CVE) would be "the most logical fit" to buy MEG (OTCPK:MEGEF), since both companies have operations in the same oil sands region in Alberta and could cut costs.

data.title: S&T Bancorp GAAP EPS of $0.83 beats by $0.02, revenue of $100.07M beats by $1.02M
data.description:
• S&T Bancorp press release (NASDAQ:STBA): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.83 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $100.07M (+3.3% Y/Y) beats by $1.02M.
  • Strong return metrics with return on average assets (ROA) of 1.32%, return on average equity (ROE) of 8.91% and return on average tangible equity (ROTE) (non-GAAP) of 12.12% compared to ROA of 1.41%, ROE of 9.67% and ROTE (non-GAAP) of 13.29% for the first quarter of 2025.
  • Pre-provision net revenue to average assets (PPNR) (non-GAAP) was solid at 1.73% for both the second and first quarters of 2025.
  • Net interest income growth of $3.3 million, or 3.90%, and net interest margin on a fully taxable equivalent basis (NIM) (FTE) (non-GAAP) expansion of 7 basis points to 3.88% compared to 3.81% in the first quarter of 2025.
  • Total portfolio loans increased $98.1 million, or 5.02% annualized, compared to March 31, 2025.
  • Total deposits increased $28.0 million, or 1.42% annualized, compared to March 31, 2025.
  • Nonperforming assets decreased $1.1 million to $21.3 million, or 0.27% of total loans plus other real estate owned (OREO), compared to $22.4 million, or 0.29%, at March 31, 2025.

data.title: Blackstone in charts: Q2 fee-related earnings growth jumps Y/Y
PHINIA Non-GAAP EPS of $1.27 beats by $0.24, revenue of $890M beats by $30.16M
data.description:
• PHINIA press release (NYSE:PHIN): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.27 beats by $0.24.
  • Revenue of $890M (+2.5% Y/Y) beats by $30.16M.
  • The Company refined its expected 2025 net sales to $3.33 billion to $3.43 billion vs $3.33B consensus

  • The Company’s net earnings and adjusted EBITDA are projected to be $140 million to $170 million and $455 million to $485 million, respectively, with net earnings margin of 4.2% to 5.0% and adjusted EBITDA margin of 13.7% to 14.1%.

  • The Company expects to generate $160 million to $200 million in adjusted free cash flow. Adjusted tax rate is expected to be in the range of 36% to 40%.
